headline: Bologna, a symbol of progressive Italy, is targeted by the far right
description: Heir to humanist ideas and an antifascist culture established after World War II, the capital of Emilia-Romagna has become a laboratory for resistance to reactionary ideas in Italy.

headline: White House correspondents' dinner suspect charged with attempted assassination of Trump
Elon Musk vs. Sam Altman: Silicon Valley's AI mavericks face off in court
UK PM Starmer faces possible parliament probe over Mandelson
description: In the latest development of an unrelenting scandal revolving around Peter Mandelson, the disgraced British politician accused of misconduct linked to convicted sex criminal Jeffrey Epstein, Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces a potential parliamentary probe over his decision to appoint Mandelson.

headline: Sarah Rey, historian: 'The far right loves antiquity, which never asked for it'
description: In an op-ed, the academic and specialist in ancient history traces the genealogy of the far right's appropriation of Greco-Roman references and examines the ideological motivations behind this political choice.

headline: 30 cents for an apartment with a view? Egypt says no more as it ends old rent law
description: For decades, millions of Egyptians have paid tiny rents thanks to a law that froze prices. A reform championed by President Abdel Fattah el-Sissi is now set to challenge this pillar of the social contract.

headline: Global backslide in women's rights did not begin with Trump
description: On March 9, the US refused to sign off on the conclusions of the UN Commission on the Status of Women, the main global women's rights forum. This was the first time since the forum's creation in 1946, and it reflects the growing threat that conservative ideologies pose to feminist achievements.

headline: Pillars of nuclear non-proliferation treaty are being undermined
Charles III visits the US in bid to repair the 'special relationship' between London and Washington
Trump discussed Iran's proposal to reopen Strait of Hormuz with his team
Brazil's 'lithium valley' fails to deliver on its promise to lift residents out of poverty
Mali junta more vulnerable than ever after weekend attacks
Taylor Swift files to trademark her voice amid AI clone boom
Empty homes: Paris targets owners' wallets, following Brussels and New York's lead
Claire's shuts all 154 stores in UK and Ireland
Iranian FM blames US for failure of talks while in Russia for diplomatic tour
France to reimburse cadmium tests, but doctors warn access is 'too restrictive'
Colombia summit brings together 50 countries to discuss phasing out fossil fuels
Hungary: 'After 16 years under an illiberal regime, can the rule of law be restored if it's set aside, even briefly?'
40 years after Chernobyl: 'All information related to the accident was classified as top secret'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, historian Galia Ackerman describes what happened before the Chernobyl disaster and the incompetence of the Soviet regime, which deliberately hid the truth about the catastrophe's severity from the public.

headline: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
description: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.

headline: Should fiction be faithful to history? Film director Xavier Giannoli and historian Tal Bruttmann discuss
description: Xavier Giannoli's latest film, 'Les Rayons et les Ombres,' is a critical and box office success. It has also sparked questions about its approach to the character of Jean Luchaire, a collaborator during World War II. Can a work of art take liberties with the facts, especially when dealing with such a sensitive period?

headline: A late but essential response to France's housing crisis
'We must never forget why the peace agreement signed between Israel and Lebanon in 1983 collapsed less than a year later'
French Socialists' policy platform: Neither new nor complete, but at least it exists
